energy about 3 MeV. Air-cooled Ti-T target, Ti-layer
of 11 mm diameter, 10.1 mg/cm**2 thickness, on copper
backing of 0.5mm thickness, mounted in thin copper
chamber (circular tube of 70 mm long, 14mm outer
diameter, 1 mm thick wall, end plate of 0.5 mm thick).

Neutron intensity was monitored with NE-213 detector.
with a set of standard gamma-ray source. For gamma-ray
Sample set was composed of two Al foils sandwiched
between Nb foils with Au foil opposite the target.
1-st series of irradiations for c-s ratios:
Neutron energy 19.9-12.8 MeV, angle for irradiation
0-140 deg., d+ energy 3.26MeV, beam current 10 microA,
Angular spectra of source neutrons were
measured by TOF technique at angles from 0 to 150 deg
Nuclear Data Guide for Reactor Neutron Metrology,
Cross sections ratio was derived from ratio of
- Fluctuation of neutron flux during irradiation.
- Difference of neutron flux between foils.
- Contribution of parasitic D(d,n)He-3 neutrons to
- Self-absorption of gamma-rays in the foils.
- Sum-coincidence effect of cascade gamma-rays for Al
samples was evaluated experimentally to be less 1%,
was included in uncertainties of efficiencies..
